'''''Star Wars'' 13''' is the thirteenth issue in the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series of comics. It was published on [[December 22]], [[1999]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''When conflict threatens to erupt somewhere in the galaxy, Jedi Knights are dispatched to mediate, uphold righteousness, and defend the weak. But if the potential conflict threatens to explode into full-scale galactic war, the Jedi Masters themselves must be on hand. Unfortunately, a web of deceit on a grand scale awaits Ki-Adi-Mundi, his new apprentice A'Sharad Hett, and the rest of the Jedi Council on the high-speed Podracing tracks of Malastare!''

==Plot summary==
This arc opens with a training session of A'sharad Hett by the [[The Dark Woman|Dark Woman]]. Shortly after, Ki-Adi-Mundi is called to a session of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. The matter at hand: insurgents on the planet [[Lannik/Legends|Lannik]] are finally willing to begin [[Lannik–Red Iaro peace negotiations|peace talks]]. The talks are to take place on the planet [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]], in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Six members of the Council are chosen to go as mediators: Ki-Adi-Mundi, [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell]], [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]], and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]. As Mace Windu and [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] complete the [[Lightsaber/Legends#Concordance of Fealty|Concordance of Fealty]], Ki-Adi-Mundi finds his Padawan talking with a young [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]].

==Continuity==
A'Sharad Hett's meeting with Anakin on the balcony at the Jedi Temple is expanded upon in the young adult novel ''[[The Life and Legend of Obi-Wan Kenobi]]''.

The [[Concordance of Fealty]] was a [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] to explain the difference between Mace Windu's two lightsabers, the one that appears in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']], and the one that comes with the [[Hasbro]] action figure.
